name: Pla de Mallorca Gravel Loop from Palma
name_original: Ruta gravel desde Palma a Santa Maria del Camí
description: This difficult 56.9-mile gravel ride from Palma to Santa Maria del Camí offers diverse terrain and vineyard views for fit cyclists.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Palma offers diverse landscapes, ranging from the rugged Serra de Tramuntana mountains to the expansive central plains of Pla de Mallorca. The region features a mix of dirt roads, trails, and coastal paths, providing varied terrain for cyclists. This includes challenging mountain ascents, rolling countryside routes through olive groves and vineyards, and flatter coastal sections. Best gravel bike trails around Palma The most popular gravel bike trail is Pla de Mallorca Gravel Loop from Palma, a 56.9 miles (91.6 km) trail that takes 6 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route explores the central plains, offering diverse terrain and vineyard views. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Drinking Water Fountain – Palma to Bunyola Road loop from Plaça d'Espanya, a difficult 29.0 miles (46.7 km) path. This route features challenging climbs and scenic roads towards the mountains. Local gravel bikers also love the Santa Maria del Camí – Quiet Country Road loop from Son Fuster, a 45.0 miles (72.5 km) trail leading through quiet country roads and olive groves, often completed in about 5 hours 7 minutes. Gravel biking around Palma is defined by rugged mountains, rolling countryside, and coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to smoother country roads. The routes in Palma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 300 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Palma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Palma?
A: Gravel biking around Palma offers a diverse mix of landscapes. You'll find rugged dirt roads and trails in the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Tramuntana mountains&lt;/strong&gt;, expansive networks of dirt roads through olive groves and vineyards in the central &lt;strong&gt;Pla de Mallorca&lt;/strong&gt;, and flatter coastal paths with beautiful sea views. The routes can range from technical descents and challenging climbs to leisurely country roads.

Q: When is the best time of year to go gravel biking in Palma?
A: The mild climate of Mallorca makes it an ideal year-round cycling destination, but &lt;strong&gt;spring and autumn&lt;/strong&gt; are particularly recommended. During these seasons, the weather is pleasant, and you can avoid the peak summer heat, making for more comfortable rides.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly gravel bike trails near Palma?
A: Yes, there are options suitable for beginners and families. The &lt;strong&gt;S'Albufera Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; offers an easy gravel route through wetlands. Another excellent choice is the "Via Verde" bike path, a converted railway line from Manacor to Artà, which is flat and suitable for all cycling levels. While not explicitly easy, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39562991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria del Camí – Cami de Muro (Vineyard Route) loop from Jacint Verdaguer&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option with less elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging gravel bike routes around Palma?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Tramuntana mountains&lt;/strong&gt; offer routes with significant climbs and technical sections. A difficult route popular with local gravel bikers is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38139760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Drinking Water Fountain – Palma to Bunyola Road loop from Plaça d'Espanya&lt;/a&gt;, which features challenging climbs and scenic roads towards the mountains. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Valldemossa – Ascent to Galilea loop from Comellar d'en Fornés&lt;/a&gt;, which includes nearly 2000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular gravel bike routes in Palma?
A: Yes, many gravel routes around Palma are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1671595378/pla-de-mallorca-gravel-loop-from-palma" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pla de Mallorca Gravel Loop from Palma&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the central plains. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31032243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria del Camí – Quiet Country Road loop from Son Fuster&lt;/a&gt;, taking you through quiet country roads and olive groves.

Q: What kind of natural features and viewpoints can I see along the gravel trails?
A: Mallorca's gravel routes offer stunning natural beauty and panoramic views. You can expect to see ancient olive groves, vineyards, and coastal vistas. Routes in the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Tramuntana&lt;/strong&gt; provide spectacular views of both mountains and the Mediterranean Sea. The &lt;strong&gt;Puig de Randa&lt;/strong&gt; offers incredible views across the entire island from its summit, and the &lt;strong&gt;Cap de Formentor&lt;/strong&gt; provides breathtaking sea views.

Q: Are there any historical sites or interesting landmarks along the routes?
A: Many gravel routes pass through charming Mallorcan towns and offer opportunities to explore local culture and history. For instance, the climb to &lt;strong&gt;Coll de Randa&lt;/strong&gt; leads to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1328710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuari de Cura&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient Christian holy site. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/117529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galatzó Public Estate&lt;/a&gt; or even ancient charcoal burner's hut ruins like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6457707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of a Historic Charcoal Burner's Hut&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any caves I can explore near the gravel biking areas?
A: Yes, the region around Palma features several interesting caves. You might find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillem Hermitage Cave&lt;/a&gt; or the more well-known &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gènova Caves&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes might also pass by smaller natural formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covota of Puig Gros de Bendinat&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about gravel biking in Palma?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.3 stars from over 10 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Reviewers often praise the incredible variety of terrain, from rugged mountains to quiet country roads, and the stunning panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and diverse landscapes. The opportunity to explore charming villages and natural parks away from typical tourist spots is also frequently highlighted.

Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Palma?
A: There are over &lt;strong&gt;30 gravel bike trails&lt;/strong&gt; available around Palma, catering to various skill levels. These include a mix of moderate and difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every gravel biking enthusiast.

Q: Are there opportunities for cafe stops or places to rest along the routes?
A: Many gravel routes lead through or near charming Mallorcan towns and villages, providing ample opportunities for cafe stops, refreshments, and experiencing local culture. The central plains, in particular, are dotted with small towns where you can find places to rest and refuel.
tips: This trail is one of my favorites. Suitable for road bikes but more enjoyed in gravelbike. It has connections with other trails towards Algaida.

From Santa Maria to Costitx you can find different gravel trails, some are private but most are open and connect to each other. Those of Santa Maria are like a maze getting them to be fun and you never see them the same.
Very picturesque town in which every Sunday there is an organic market with high-quality regional products, highly recommended!

faqs.question: How difficult is this gravel ride?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a "Hard gravel ride" and requires "very good fitness." It spans approximately 91.6 kilometers with an elevation gain of around 420 meters. Some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride, indicating potentially rough or technical sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses varied surfaces, including red dirt roads, stone roads, sandy roads, and shaded, mossy asphalt roads. As you approach Santa Maria del Camí, the scenery opens up to picturesque vineyards and farmlands, offering the rugged beauty of the region and quiet countryside lanes.

faqs.question: What type of bike and tires are recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: While some parts of Mallorca's gravel can be smooth enough for road bikes with narrow tires, tires between 35-42 mm are generally recommended for most gravel routes on the island, including this one, to ensure a more enjoyable and comfortable experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this gravel ride?
faqs.answer: The route starts in Palma. Specific parking information for the trailhead is not detailed, but you would typically look for public parking options within Palma that allow for bike unloading. Consider starting points that offer easy access to the initial segments of the route.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Palma is well-connected by public transport. You would need to check local bus or train services from your location to Palma. If bringing a bike, verify the specific public transport provider's policy on carrying bicycles, as this can vary.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route features several notable points, including the Sa Bassa Leal Gravel Route and a scenic Gravel Path Through the Vineyards of Santa Maria. The destination, Santa Maria del Camí, is also a highlight, known for its agricultural setting.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this gravel route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during many seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for cycling in Mallorca, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. This allows for comfortable riding conditions through the diverse landscapes.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a "Hard gravel ride" requiring "very good fitness." It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children due to its length, elevation gain, and potentially rough or technical sections.

faqs.question: Is the Gravel ride from Palma to Santa Maria del Camí dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular route are not provided, generally, dogs are allowed on many public trails in Mallorca if kept under control, often on a leash. However, given the route's length and difficulty, consider if it's appropriate for your dog's fitness level.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this gravel route from Palma to Santa Maria del Camí. It utilizes public roads and trails.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the El Camí IT4TR4 El Camí Illenc: de l'Alguer a Formentera, camí de Montuïri, and camí de Son Company, among others, offering connections to a broader network of trails.

waypoints.name: Gravel Path Through the Vineyards of Santa Maria
